  • homicsx.geometry.universal_generator

Source code for homicsx.geometry.universal_generator

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any

import numpy as np

from homicsx.core.geometry import (
    RVEGeometry, 
    GeometryInput,
)

from .generators_3d import generate_mono_3d, generate_poly_3d
from .generators_2d import generate_mono_2d, generate_poly_2d




[docs]
def patriculate_geometry_generator(
        input_data: GeometryInput
) -> RVEGeometry:
    """
    Main entry point for generating inclusion-based Representative Volume Element (RVE) geometries.

    This function acts as a dispatcher that routes the generation 
    request to the appropriate specialized generator based on the spatial 
    dimension (2D/3D) and the particle size distribution (mono/polydisperse).

    Parameters
    ----------
    input_data : GeometryInput
        A structured data object containing 
        all necessary parameters including:
        - dim: Spatial dimension (2 or 3).
        - dispersion: Type of size distribution ("mono" or "poly").
        - volume_fraction: Target packing density.
        - domain_size: RVE dimensions.
        - Other geometry-specific settings.

    Returns
    -------
    RVEGeometry
        An object containing the generated microstructure data, typically including 
        particle centers, axes, shapes, and potentially periodic images.
    
    Notes
    -----
    The generator follows the RSA (Random Sequential Adsorption) approach 
    internally within the specialized sub-functions to ensure non-overlapping 
    placements.

    See also
    --------
    GeometryInput
    """
    if input_data.dim == 3:
        if input_data.dispersion == "mono":
            return generate_mono_3d(input_data)
        elif input_data.dispersion == "poly":
            return generate_poly_3d(input_data)
            
    elif input_data.dim == 2:
        if input_data.dispersion == "mono":
            return generate_mono_2d(input_data)
        elif input_data.dispersion == "poly":
            return generate_poly_2d(input_data)


         

__all__ = [
    # universal_generator
    "patriculate_geometry_generator",
]
